questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
If $f(x)$ is a polynomial function such that $f(x)+f^{\prime}(x)+f^{\prime \prime}(x)+f^{\prime \prime \prime}(x)=x^3$, $g(x)=\int \frac{f(x)}{x^3} d x$ and $g(1)=1$, then:
[Note: Where $[k]$ denotes greatest integer function less than or equal to $k$.]
options: the value of $\lim _{x \rightarrow 1}(g(x))^{\frac{1}{x-1}}$ is equal to $e^2$
number of solution of the equation $g(x)=0$ is 2
the value of $[g(e)]$ is equal to -1
questions.solution:
Let, $f(x)=x^3+a x^2+b x+c$, then
$\begin{aligned} & f(x)=x^3+a x^2+b x+c \\ & f^{\prime}(x)=3 x^2+2 a x+b \\ & f^{\prime \prime}(x)=6 x+2 \\ & f^{\prime \prime \prime}(x)=6\end{aligned}$
Equating the coefficient of $f(x)+f^{\prime}(x)+f^{\prime \prime}(x)+f^{\prime \prime \prime}(x)=x^3$
We have
$3+a=0 \quad \Rightarrow \quad a=-3$
$b+2 a+6=0 \quad \Rightarrow \quad b=0$
and  $c+b+2 a+6=0 \quad \Rightarrow \quad c=0$
Therefore, $\quad f(x)=x^3-3 x^2$ and
$g(x)=\int \frac{f(x)}{x^3} d x=\int \frac{x^3-3 x^2}{x^3} d x=\int\left(1-\frac{3}{x}\right) d x=x-3 \ln x+C$
Where, $C$ is the constant of integration
$g(1)=1-3 \ln 1+C=1 \quad \Rightarrow \quad C=0$
$\Rightarrow \quad g(x)=x-3 \ln x$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Let $\int e^{x^2} \cdot e^x\left(2 x^2+x+1\right) d x=e^{x^2} \cdot f(x)+C$ where $f(x)$ is some non-zero constant function and $C$ is some arbitrary constant. If the local minimum value of $f(x)$ is equal to $m$, then:
[Note: [ ] denotes greatest integer function.]
options: the value of $\lim _{x \rightarrow 0}(1+f(x))^{1 / x}$ is equal to 1
the value of $\int_0^1\left(f(x)+e^x\right) d x$ is equal to $2 e$.
the value of $\left[\frac{-1}{m}\right]$ is equal to 2 .
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \int \underbrace{x}_{\mathrm{I}} \cdot \underbrace{e^{x^2+x} \cdot(2 x+1)}_{\text {II }} d x+\int e^{x^2+x} d x \\ & x \cdot e^{x^2+x}+\int e^{x^2+x} d x\end{aligned}$
$\therefore \quad f(x)=x e^x$
$\begin{aligned} & f^{\prime}(x)=e^x(1+x)=0 \quad \Rightarrow x=-1 \\ & f^{\prime \prime}(x)=e^x(2+x) \\ & f^{\prime \prime}(-1)=0 \Rightarrow \text { Minima }\end{aligned}$
$\therefore \quad f(-1)=\frac{-1}{e}=m$
$\left[\frac{-1}{m}\right]=[e]=2$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $\int \frac{(x-1) d x}{x^2 \sqrt{2 x^2-2 x+1}}=\frac{\sqrt{f(x)}}{g(x)}+c$ then
questions.solution:
$1=\int \frac{x-1}{x^3 \sqrt{2-\frac{2}{x}+\frac{1}{x^2}}} d x$
Put $2-\frac{2}{x}+\frac{1}{x^2}=t^2$
$\Rightarrow\left(\frac{2}{x^2}-\frac{2}{x^3}\right) d x=2 t d t$
$\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\int_1^{\mathrm{t}} \mathrm{dt}$
$I=t+c$
$I=\sqrt{2-\frac{2}{x}+\frac{1}{x^2}}+c$
$1=\frac{\sqrt{2 x^2-2 x+1}}{x}+c$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
options: $f(x)$ has at least one rela root
questions.solution:
$f(x)=\int \frac{x^8+4}{x^4-2 x^2+2} d x, f(0)=0$
$=\int \frac{x^8+4+4 x^4-4 x^4}{x^4-2 x^2+2} d x=\int \frac{\left(x^4+2 x^2+2\right)\left(x^4-2 x^2+2\right)}{\left(x^4-2 x^2+2\right)} d x=\frac{x^5}{5}+\frac{2}{3} x^3+2 x+c$
$f(0)=0+c=0 \Rightarrow c=0$
$\therefore f(x)=\frac{x^5}{5}+\frac{2 x^3}{3}+2 x$
$\therefore f(x)$ is an odd function, its range is $R$, has at least one real root, is monotonic

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $\int \frac{3 x \sin ^2 x \cos x-3 \sin ^3 x}{x^4} d x=f(x)+C$, where $\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} f(x)=1$ and $C$ is the constant of integration, then:
options: the value of $\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{\int_0^x t f(t) d t-2 x^2}{1-\cos x}=-3$
if $h(x)=x \cdot \sqrt[3]{f(x)}$, then $\int_0^{\frac{\pi}{2}} e^{h(x)}\left(\cos ^2 x-\sin x\right) d x=-1$
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \int \frac{3 \sin ^2 x \cos x}{x^3} d x-3 \int \frac{\sin ^3 x}{x^4} d x \\ & =\int \frac{3 \sin ^2 x \cos x}{x^3} d x-3\left(\sin ^3 x\left(\frac{-1}{3 x^3}\right)-\int 3 \sin ^2 x \cos x\left(\frac{-1}{3 x^3}\right) d x\right)+C=\frac{\sin ^3 x}{x^3}+C\end{aligned}$
$\therefore \quad f(x)=\frac{\sin ^3 x}{x^3}$
(1) $\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{\int_0^x t \cdot \frac{\sin ^3 t}{t^3} d t-2 x^2}{1-\cos x}=\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{\frac{\sin ^3 x}{x^2}-4 x}{\sin x}=\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{\frac{\sin ^3 x}{x^3}-4}{\frac{\sin x}{x}}=-3$
(2) $\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{\frac{\sin x}{x}-x^2}{x^2}=\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{\sin x-x^3}{x^3}=$ D.N.E.
(3) $h(x)=x \cdot \frac{\sin x}{x}=\sin x \quad \Rightarrow \quad \int_0^\pi \sin ^4 d x=\frac{3 \pi}{8}$
(4) $\int_0^{\frac{\pi}{2}} e^{\sin x}(\cos x \cos x+(-\sin x)) d x=\left(e^{\sin x} \cdot \cos x\right)_0^{\pi / 2}=0-1$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $I=\int \frac{x^2}{(x \sin x+\cos x)^2} d x$. Which of the following options is equivalent to the given indefinite integral (ignoring arbitrary constant)?
options: $I=\frac{x \sec x}{x \sin x+\cos x}-\int \frac{\sec x(1+x \tan x)}{x \sin x+\cos x} d x$
$I=\frac{\sec x(1+x \tan x)}{x \sin x+\cos x} d x-\frac{x \sec x}{x \sin x+\cos x}$
questions.solution:
$I=\int \frac{x^2}{(x \sin x+\cos x)^2} d x$
Put $x^2=x \sec x \cdot x \cos x$
$I=\int \frac{x \sec x \cdot x \cos x}{(x \sin x+\cos x)^2} d x$
Integrating by parts taking $x \sec x$ as first function and $\frac{x \cos x}{(x \sin x+\cos x)^2}$ as second function, we get
$I=x \sec x \int \frac{x \cos x}{(x \sin x+\cos x)^2} d x+\int(x \sec x \tan x+\sec x)\left(\int \frac{x \cos x}{(x \sin x+\cos x)^2} d x\right) d x$  ...(1)
$I_1=\int \frac{x \cos x}{(x \sin x+\cos x)^2} d x$
Put $x \sin x+\cos x=t \quad \Rightarrow \quad x \cos x d x=d t$
$\Rightarrow \quad I_1=\int \frac{1}{t^2} d t \quad \Rightarrow \quad I_1=\frac{-1}{t}$
(Ignoring arbitrary constant as per the question)
$\Rightarrow \quad I_1=\frac{-1}{x \cos x+\sin x}$
Putting value of $I_1$ in (1), we get
$I=\frac{-x \sec x}{x \sin x+\cos x}+\int \frac{\sec x(1+\tan x)}{x \sin x+\cos x} d x$
which is the option d.
Simplifying this further by taking $\cos x$ common in denominator in the second term we get
$I=\frac{-x \sec x}{x \sin x+\cos x}+\int \frac{\sec x(1+x \tan x)}{\cos x(1+x \tan x)} d x$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $\int\left(x^2-1\right) e^x\left(x^2+4 x+1\right) d x=e^x f(x)+C$ (where $C$ is constant of integration). If $g(x)=f(x)+f^{\prime}(x)$, then:
options: number of integral roots of $g(x)=0$ is 2 .
sum of square of integral roots of $g(x)=0$ is 2 .
if $\alpha$ is one non-integral root of $g(x)=0$, then $\alpha^4+4 \alpha^3+2 \alpha^2+4 \alpha+2$ is equal to 1 .
questions.solution:
Differentiating w.r.t. $x$, we get
$\left(x^2-1\right) e^x\left(x^2+4 x+1\right)=e^x\left(f(x)+f^{\prime}(x)\right)$
$\Rightarrow \quad g(x)=f(x)+f^{\prime}(x)=\left(x^2-1\right)\left(x^2+4 x+1\right)$
$g(x)=0 \Rightarrow x= \pm 1 \quad$ or $\quad x^2+4 x+1=0$
$\begin{array}{ll}\therefore & \alpha^2+4 \alpha+1=0 \\ \therefore & \text { Given expression }=\left(\alpha^2+4 \alpha+1\right)\left(\alpha^2+1\right)+1=1 .\end{array}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.solution:
$1=\int\left(3 \sin \theta-4 \sin ^3 \theta\right) e^{\sin \theta} \cos \theta d \theta=\left(A \sin ^3 \theta-B \sin ^2 \theta+C \sin \theta+D \cos \theta+B+E\right) e^{\mathrm{ven} \theta}+F$
Taking $\sin \theta=\mathrm{t}$
$I=\int\left(3 t-4 t^3\right) e^t d t=\underbrace{\left(A t^3-B t^2+C t+B+E\right)}_{f(t)} e^t+F$
$\begin{aligned} & D=0 \text { as } f(t) \text { is a polynomial in } t \\ & \left(3 t-4 t^3\right) e^t=\left(A t^3-B t^2+C t+B+E\right) e^t+\left(3 \mathrm{At}^2-2 B t+C\right) e^t \\ & A=-4 \\ & 3 A=B \Rightarrow B=-12\end{aligned}$
